demo.name: NixOS/nixpkgs
demo.teaser: Massive infra codebase with broad subsystem ownership and frequent coordination churn.

metrics.label: Maintainer concentration
metrics.note: Top 5 maintainers touch 61% of critical platform paths.

metrics.label: Contributor expertise signals
metrics.note: Fourteen contributors show repeated authorship in networking, NixOS modules, and release plumbing.

metrics.label: Subsystem hotspots
metrics.note: Networking, stdenv, and module evaluation dominate recent coordination cost.

dashboard.headline: Operational complexity with concentrated review bottlenecks.
dashboard.narrative: Vaglio highlights which maintainers absorb the coordination load, where routing and platform changes pile up, and how much of the rollout reasoning is already captured versus still trapped in chat.

history.note: Routing and DNS changes begin to overlap in the same rollout slice.
Evaluation failures and rollback planning stack on the same branch.
Operational notes land, but a few high-cost review surfaces remain contested.

metrics.label: Branch stress
metrics.note: The HA router rollout branch bundles multiple tightly coupled infra surfaces.

metrics.label: History heat
metrics.note: Networking, module evaluation, and release plumbing each show repeated stress spikes.

metrics.label: Active-inference confidence
metrics.note: Enough structured evidence exists to rank hotspots, but not yet enough provenance to collapse uncertainty.

stress.headline: Prediction error clusters around infra-wide rollout paths.
stress.narrative: The first-pass stress model treats contested, high-blast-radius paths as high prediction error and stable, well-vouched paths as low prediction error. It complements raw churn by showing where coordination pressure is compounding rather than merely changing.

hotspots.title: router failover path
hotspots.detail: High precision operators disagree across firewall, DHCP, and DNS coupling, so contention remains costly even when code size is modest.

hotspots.title: module evaluation path
hotspots.detail: Repeated evaluation regressions signal unresolved model mismatch rather than one-off patch churn.

hotspots.title: release plumbing path
hotspots.detail: Rollout notes and provenance reduce surprise, but the path still spikes when fixes arrive under time pressure.

hotspots.note: Changes to HA failover, firewalling, and DNS overlap across multiple machines.
High reviewer load spikes when infra-wide options change.
Operational fixes often need provenance and rollout notes, not just code diffs.

expertise_signals.title: Infrastructure stewards
expertise_signals.detail: Repeat edits across router, secrets, and NixOS module surfaces signal high operational context.

expertise_signals.title: Evaluation specialists
expertise_signals.detail: A smaller group consistently resolves failures tied to module graph and build-system edges.

provenance.title: Deliberation overlay
provenance.detail: Link HA router changes to the exact roundtable discussions and rollout rationale that shaped them.

provenance.title: JJ-native review layer
provenance.detail: Keep Git/Forgejo at the edge while preserving `jj`-level change identity for agent-heavy work.

shell_inputs.pull_title: HA router and DNS failover hardening

import_steps.detail: Mirror NixOS/nixpkgs into a Forgejo-hosted demo namespace without bespoke setup.

import_steps.step: Translate Git edge to jj semantics
import_steps.detail: Project refs, pull requests, and merges through the Git↔jj gateway before analysis.
Expose maintainer concentration, expertise signals, hotspots, provenance overlays, and the first stress/heat surface on the same host.
